reference: Liang et al. (2022). Holistic Evaluation of Language Models. TMLR 2023.
key_limitations: Model-centric design — evaluates the LLM in isolation, not the integrated system
No system integration metrics — IQ dimension almost entirely uncovered
No transparency/explainability metrics — TI dimension severely underserved
No production-context safety monitoring — SS metrics only at model benchmark level
Benchmark performance is poor predictor of deployment behavior (per [14] Guo et al. 2024)
No context window / RAG evaluation — retrieval pipeline quality invisible
No prompt injection resistance evaluation at system level
No hallucination detection in production context — only benchmark-level truthfulness
No latency SLA or API reliability monitoring
Static benchmark evaluation — no continuous/longitudinal monitoring capability
strengths: Strong coverage of Functional Correctness (FC) dimension
Robustness evaluation via adversarial benchmarks (AdvGLUE, ANLI)
adaptation_notes: HELM applied to isolated model queries representative of each system's use cases. Per-scenario scores mapped to QALIS dimensions using the scenario-to-dimension mapping table.
